text.title: domain-driven-design Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the domain-driven-design methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Domain logic lives INSIDE the model (entities/aggregates), not in services. An Order that cannot be placed empty is safer than a service that checks emptiness before calling place().
rule.rationale: Logic in services scatters and breaks silently when one copy is missed.

rule.statement: Use the ubiquitous language consistently in code: class names, method names, module names mirror the business terms.
rule.rationale: Without naming alignment, dev-talk and business-talk diverge.

rule.statement: Bounded contexts are explicit. A term may mean different things in different contexts; the model in each context owns its semantics.
rule.rationale: Implicit boundaries fail at service-split time.

rule.statement: Aggregate roots are the only entry point to their cluster. Repositories return aggregate roots, not arbitrary entities.
rule.rationale: Bypassing the aggregate root bypasses its invariants.

rule.statement: Value objects are immutable + identity-by-value (equality by attributes, not by reference).
rule.rationale: Mutable value objects cause aliasing bugs.

rule.statement: A context map between bounded contexts is explicit (upstream/downstream/anti-corruption-layer) and committed to docs.
rule.rationale: Implicit context relationships lead to model leaks.

rule.statement: Skip when the input does not satisfy the Applies-If preconditions or another methodology owns this output shape.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work and wrong-tool application.
